title: Conflict Resolution: Staging Merge Verification for helix-cli
description: This tour walks through the conflict resolution run for the helix-cli repository after a staging refresh merge. The sole conflicted file was src/tickets/index.ts, which the auto-merge resolved cleanly with zero conflict markers remaining. No source code changes were required. The tour verifies that all 10 ticket subcommands are intact in the ticket dispatcher, the library CLI module (6 files in src/library/) is unaffected, and the TypeScript build passes with zero errors. Each stop explains the diagnostic evidence gathered and why no manual intervention was needed.

steps.description: The staging refresh merge introduced a conflict in src/tickets/index.ts — the ticket subcommand dispatcher. Four ticket-side commits (5cfbd79, ca6c51b, 210d9fc, 758fce3, covering library CLI integration and ticket lookup improvements) conflicted with one staging-side commit (6a4215c, concurrent ticket command updates). Git's auto-merge resolved the conflict cleanly. This diagnosis document confirms zero conflict markers remain and the library module in src/library/ was entirely separate from the conflict, leaving it unaffected.
steps.title: Overview: What Was the Conflict?

steps.description: The APL for diagnosis asked two questions: (1) Does tickets/index.ts still have active conflict markers? Answer: No — grep for '<<<<<<' returned zero matches and the file is 150 clean lines with all 10 ticket subcommands present. (2) Is the library module intact? Answer: Yes — merge-conflicts.json listed only tickets/index.ts, meaning all 6 library files (src/library/index.ts, list.ts, show.ts, comments.ts, comments-list.ts, comments-post.ts) were never touched by the conflict. This framed the implementation plan: no source changes needed, only build verification.
steps.title: Diagnosis: Two Key Questions Answered

steps.description: Because the auto-merge resolved cleanly, the implementation plan required only three steps: (1) grep for conflict markers repo-wide to confirm zero remain, (2) run npm install to bring node_modules up to date, and (3) run npm run build (which invokes tsc) as the primary correctness gate. The plan explicitly states no source code changes are needed and that all 6 library command files were only on the ticket side and not involved in any conflict. The TypeScript build serves as the integration test catching any import or type breakages introduced by the merge.
steps.title: Implementation Plan: Verify, Install, Build

steps.description: The implementation plan APL confirmed two of three questions with evidence: tickets/index.ts has all 10 imports and switch cases (no markers), and no library files were involved in any conflict. Notably, Q3 — whether the TypeScript build passes — was marked 'not yet verified in this run,' flagging it as the primary action for the implementation step (CHK-02). This honest acknowledgment of what was still pending ensured the implementation step would not skip the build check.
steps.title: Implementation Plan APL: Build Not Yet Verified

steps.description: The implementation APL records the execution results. All three questions resolved positively: (1) Zero conflict markers in tickets/index.ts and across all src/ files — confirmed by grep. (2) tsc completed with exit code 0 and zero errors, triggered via the npm install prepare script. (3) All 10 ticket subcommand imports (list, latest, get, create, rerun, continue, artifacts, artifact, bundle, update-description) are present at lines 4-13, and the library module is registered in src/index.ts at lines 14, 54-57, and 98. Data flow is intact end-to-end.
steps.title: Implementation: Build Passed, All Intents Preserved

steps.description: The code review examined all 8 files in scope. Each library file was verified individually: src/library/index.ts routes list/show/comments subcommands; list.ts produces tabular output; show.ts annotates headings with rating counts; comments.ts routes list and post; comments-list.ts supports optional --section filter; comments-post.ts handles rating aliases and --reply-to. src/index.ts has the library module registered at three points (import line 14, help text lines 54-57, case handler line 98). SKILL.md documents all 4 library commands with usage examples. No issues found.
steps.title: Code Review: All Library Commands Verified

steps.description: The code review APL provides the final confirmation: all 6 library source files are present and functional, the library module is registered at all three required points in src/index.ts, and SKILL.md documents all 4 library commands (list, show, comments list, comments post) with usage examples and --reply-to support. The APL statement reads: 'CLI review confirmed all library commands intact after staging merge. No code changes required.' The codebase's major code paths — ticket subcommand dispatch and library command routing — are both clean and verified by a passing TypeScript build.
steps.title: Summary: Conflict Resolution Complete, No Changes Required
